raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Senate Bill 276 supports low-to-moderate income working families who qualify for the California Earned Income Tax Credit.",
    "SB 276 authorizes taxpayers to choose between their 2019, 2020, and 2021 incomes when filing for the credit.",
    "The COVID-19 pandemic and resulting shutdowns disproportionately impacted low-income earners in California.",
    "Families who receive the California Earned Income Tax Credit often spend the refund on essential needs.",
    "According to the Center for Social Development, 78% of tax credit recipients devote a portion of their refund on housing and food.",
    "66% of individuals receiving the tax credit spend a part of their refund on school supplies, clothing, utility bills, and paying down debts.",
    "By allowing taxpayers to choose their income year for the tax credit, individuals can maximize their tax refunds."
  ]
}
```
